pick.rationale: North Carolina enters with a clear analytical advantage, ranking 27th in Torvik and 29th in KenPom compared to VCU's 46-51st range, while boasting superior defensive efficiency (34th vs 82nd) that should prove decisive in a tournament setting. The Tar Heels face a significant challenge without star freshman Caleb Wilson, who led the team in scoring before his season-ending thumb surgery, and they've gone just 5-3 without him including an ACC Tournament quarterfinal loss to Clemson. However, VCU's defensive vulnerabilities (82nd in adjusted defense) will be exploited by UNC's balanced attack, and the Tar Heels' proximity advantage in Greenville, South Carolina provides a pseudo-home-court environment. VCU's A10 tournament championship and balanced scoring (eight rotation players with 18+ three-pointers) gives them upset potential, but UNC's defensive prowess and overall talent edge should prevail in a closely contested game.

result.text: UNC opens NCAA Tournament without Caleb Wilson vs. surging VCU :: WRAL.com UNC opens NCAA Tournament without Caleb Wilson vs. surging VCU No. 6 seed North Carolina begins its NCAA Tournament run Thursday against No. 11 seed VCU while adjusting to life without star freshman Caleb Wilson. The Tar Heels have gone 5-3 since Wilson’s season-ending thumb injury. Posted 3/17/2026, 12:51:55 AM Updated 3/17/2026, 4:37:53 PM Related UNC sports UNC basketball Hubert Davis college basketball NCAA Tournament March Madness Selection Sunday Hubert Davis on VCU: We'll be ready for the challenge By Mark Bergin, WRAL senior multiplatform producer North Carolina will have somewhat of a homecourt advantage in the opening weekend of the 2026 NCAA Tournament if the Tar Heels can manage without star freshman Caleb Wilson. No. 6 seed UNC (24-8) plays No. 11 seed VCU (27-7) at 6:50 p.m. ET Thursday at Bon Secours Wellness Arena in Greenville, South Carolina. The South Region game airs on TNT. Other WRAL Top Stories Wilson, an All-ACC first-team selection, is out for the season after breaking his right thumb. Wilson injured the thumb while working back from a left hand fracture. Wilson's last game was on Feb. 20. UNC is 5-3 in eight games without Wilson. "Our team will move forward," UNC coach Hubert Davis said of Wilson's injury on March 7 after the regular-season finale against Duke. Davis added, "Obviously, there's tremendous sadness for him. I've talked about how special ... obviously he's a special player, but just how remarkable of a kid and a teammate he is and the passion that he has for his teammates, for North Carolina for being on the floor, for playing in games like this. It was a dream for him to play in the ACC [Tournament] and the NCAA Tournament, and my heart is broken that he won't be able to do that." The UNC-VCU winner will play the winner of the Illinois-Penn game on Saturday in Greenville, South Carolina. The other teams in the South Region include No. 1 seed Florida, No. 2 seed Houston, No. 4 seed Nebraska, No. 5 seed Vanderbilt, No. 7 seed Saint Mary’s, No. 8 seed Clemson, No. 9 seed Iowa, No. 10 seed Texas A&M, No. 12 seed McNeese, No. 13 seed Troy, No. 15 seed, Idaho, No. 16 seed Lehigh and No. 16 seed Prairie View A&M. UNC enters the NCAA Tournament on a two-game losing streak. Thursday marks the Tar Heels’ 55th appearance in the NCAA Tournament, the second most all-time. It is the fourth NCAA Tournament appearance in five seasons under head coach Hubert Davis, who has led the Tar Heels to an 8-3 record in the NCAA Tournament. The Tar Heels fell to Duke in the regular-season finale and lost 80-79 to Clemson in the ACC quarterfinals . Meanwhile, the Rams have won 16 of their last 17 games. It includes a three-game sweep of Duquesne, Saint Joseph's and Dayton to win the A-10 title this past weekend in Pittsburgh. The freshman guard scored 17 points and pulled down 11 rebounds, both game-high marks. VCU got out to an early six point lead. A 12-2 run opened it up to a 17 point Ram advantage late in the first half. The lead was 15 at intermission as the Flyers were held to 33 perc ent shooting over those initial twenty minutes. The second half saw Anthony Grant’s team make a run. The VCU lead was down to just seven with a little under two minutes to play. The Rams closed it out, shooting 7 of 10 from the Charity stripe in the last 1:40. VCU had a 25-11 lead in fast break points. As noted, Nyk Lewis of VCU scored 17 points to lead the way. Terrence Hill jr. added 14. Javon Bennett led Dayton with 14 points, but was limited to 6 of 16 shooting from the floor. Dayton finished shooting 43% from the floor, 33% (7 of 21) from three. VCU shot 40%, 45% (11 of 24) from long distance. Dayton led 39-34 in the rebounding department. VCU was guilty of just five turnovers while the Flyers had 11. Next. VCU will face North Thursday in Greenville, S.C. The Ram s are an 11-seed in the NCAA South Region. The meeting with the Tar Heels will be the first in VCU’s basketball history. Dayton is a two seed in their NIT bracket. Because they are hosting NCAA games, the Flyers will travel to Bradley on Wednesday for their first round matchup. “To be sitting here at VCU, a program of championships, and to be adding to that legacy, I’m super grateful. And to all the players, the coaches including (former VCU mentor and Dayton coach) Anthony Grant, that came before and the administrators that built this foundation like we’re just standing, and I told Anthony this, we’re just standing on his shoulders. We’re just standing on those people’s shoulders. We’re not the greatest to be here; we’re just the latest to be here and be part of something truly, truly, truly special.’ – P

No. 6 seed North Carolina and No. 11 seed VCU play Thursday, March 19, in a March Madness NCAA Tournament first-round game . The South Region game is scheduled for 3:50 p.m. MST and can be seen on TNT ( stream with Sling TV ). Who will win the March Madness game and advance to the second round in the NCAA Tournament bracket ? Check out these March Madness picks and March Madness predictions for the men's college basketball NCAA Tournament matchup . North Carolina is a 2.5-point favorite over VCU in the game in March Madness odds provided by BetMGM Sportsbook . The Tar Heels are -150 on the moneyline. The Rams are +125. The over/under for the game is set at 153.5 points. March Madness picks: Ohio State-TCU | Nebraska-Troy | Louisville-South Florida | Wisconsin-High Point | Duke-Siena | Vanderbilt-McNeese | Michigan State-North Dakota State | Arkansas-Hawaii | North Carolina-VCU | Saint Mary's-Texas A&M | Illinois-Penn | Georgia-Saint Louis | Gonzaga-Kennesaw State | Houston-Idaho | Kentucky-Santa Clara | Texas Tech-Akron | Arizona-Long Island | Virginia-Wright State | Iowa State-Tennessee State | Alabama-Hofstra | Villanova-Utah State | Clemson-Iowa | St. John's-UNI | UCLA-UCF | Purdue-Queens | Kansas-Cal Baptist | UConn-Furman | Miami-Missouri | The Republic's score predictions Watch North Carolina vs VCU on Sling The Arizona Republic : North Carolina 77, VCU 75 Jeremy Cluff writes: "The Tar Heels are only a 2.5-point favorite in this game. VCU won the Atlantic 10 tournament and has a lot of momentum. This could be one of the most intriguing games of the first round." March Madness picks: Predictions for NCAA Tournament first-round games Dimers : North Carolina 77, VCU 75 It writes: "After extensive simulations, our model gives Virginia Commonwealth a win probability of 45%, while North Carolina has a win probability of 55%." result.text: North Carolina vs. VCU: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds | BettingPros to prevent flash of incorrect theme on page load --> Skip to main content Skip to navigation Skip to footer Click to go to homepage Picks Odds Tools Research Leaders Click to expand More menu Home Games Picks Upgrade Menu Click to Find a bet ... Contest Click to expand picks panel Click to open Sharp AI North Carolina vs. VCU: NCAA Tournament Picks & Odds by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 by BettingPros Staff | @BettingPros | Mar 15, 2026 The 2026 NCAA Tournament is officially here! The March Madness Bracket is set, and first-round NCAA Tournament matchups are in place. It’s time to make your picks and predictions for the first round of the 2026 NCAA Tournament! We’re here to help as we’ll have picks and predictions for each of the first round 2026 NCAA Tournament games. Here are our NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for North Carolina vs. VCU. NCAA Tournament Odds NCAA Tournament Pick’Em Contest NCAA Tournament March Madness Bracket Optimizer NCAA Tournament Betting Trends 2026 NCAA Tournament Picks & Predictions: North Carolina vs. VCU Let’s dive into our top NCAA Tournament picks and predictions for this opening-round matchup. 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview: North Carolina vs. VCU North Carolina 2026 NCAA Tournament Preview Everything about North Carolina’s tournament outlook is limited by Caleb Wilson’s injury. The First Team All-ACC freshman was having a historic year before surgery ended his season in February, and without him, the Tar Heels enter the tournament with consecutive losses and major questions. Without Wilson in the lineup, this squad drops from 21st overall to 32nd, per Hoop Explorer, with the defense dropping from 20th to 40th. EvanMiya dropped the Tar Heels outside his top 45 without Wilson. Since Feb. 14, UNC ranks 38th on Bart Torvik. Wilson is obviously a big loss. Henri Veesaar is the player who has to fill the void. The junior center put up 28 points and 17 rebounds in the loss to Clemson in the ACC Tournament and is a legitimate NBA-caliber interior presence who creates matchup problems by being able to score in the post, step out on the perimeter, and rebound at an elite rate. He and Seth Trimble are the most reliable contributors left. The rest of the supporting cast is a concern. The bench has combined for single-digit scoring in each of the last two games, and the second unit has not given Hubert Davis anything meaningful to work with when the starters are struggling. The three-point defense is the other genuine problem. UNC ranks outside the top 200 nationally in opponent three-point percentage, and Clemson exploited it by shooting nearly 50% from deep in the ACC quarterfinals. Any opponent that can spread the floor and shoot will find open looks against this scheme. The Tar Heels also rank 344th in field goal rate allowed with few forced turnovers, so opponents will get shots up most

arguments.query: VCU Rams "Havoc" defense press 2026 season turnovers

toolCalls.summary: Returned 5 search results for "VCU Rams "Havoc" defense press 2026 season turnovers".

result.title: Breakdown Series | Episode 26: VCU's Havoc Defense - YouTube
result.snippet: In this episode, we're breaking down Shaka Smart's Havoc Defense , the relentless pressure system that carried VCU from mid-major obscurity all the way to the Final Four.

result.title: ESPNInsider: How 'havoc' fuels VCU's success | VCU Ram Nation
result.snippet: 'Havoc' as a disruptive defense Think of Havoc's three pressure defenses as being like three devastating pitches of a baseball pitcher: " Pitch 1 is "Fist," the Rams' fastball: VCU's man-to-man full-court press is designed to put uncomfortable pressure on the opponent's ball handlers with the general idea of creating unforced turnovers .

result.title: Top Sleeper Schools to Watch in 2026 March Madness - Lineups
result.snippet: To compliment their offense, the Rams defense have excelled at smothering their opponents shots at multiple levels of the court, ranking 62nd in Opposing Effective Field Goal Percentage. While their low Rebound Rate is alarming, the Rams Havoc helps them hide it, forcing their opponents into Turnovers before getting up a a shot attempt.

result.title: Havoc Press Defense in Basketball: Information Explained
result.snippet: The havoc press defense is a basketball defensive strategy which utilizes on-ball pressure and defensive traps, particularly in the backcourt, with a main objective of influencing the offensive team to play at a faster pace than usual, which could then lead to turnovers by that same offensive team.

result.title: VCU Rams vs. Fordham Rams, Picks and Prediction for Tuesday, February 3 ...
result.snippet: Forcing turnovers has always been a staple of the VCU defense , though they rank 116th (12.5 per game) in forcing turnovers and they're not using as much pressure ( Havoc ) defense as they used to. According to KenPom, the Rams rank 48th overall, 38th in Adjusted Offensive Efficiency and 95th in Adjusted Defense . Fordham picks up big road win over GW

result.title: 2026 ACC men's basketball tournament - Wikipedia
result.snippet: The 2026 ACC men's basketball tournament was the postseason men's basketball tournament for the Atlantic Coast Conference, held at the Spectrum Center in Charlotte, North Carolina , from March 10-14, 2026 . [1][2] It was the seventy-third edition of the tournament and fifteenth edition held in Charlotte. The tournament was sponsored by T. Rowe Price. [3] The Duke Blue Devils were the reigning ...

result.snippet: DULUTH, Ga. (AP) — Taina Mair had 19 points and 12 rebounds, Delaney Thomas also scored 19 and top-seeded Duke rallied to win its second straight women's Atlantic Coast Conference Women's Basketball Tournament title, beating No. 2 seed Louisville 70-65 in overtime on Sunday.

toolCalls.summary: Fetched https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/college/acc/2026/03/12/unc-clemson-score-acc-tournament-bracket-update/89117555007/ and extracted 3000 characters of text.

result.text: UNC vs Clemson in 2026 ACC Tournament: Tar Heels don't complete comeback Skip to main content Home NCAAB NCAAW MLB Women's Sports NFL NBA NCAAF WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Olympics Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers UNC basketball can't complete comeback vs Clemson in ACC tournament Rodd Baxley Fayetteville Observer CHARLOTTE — UNC basketball is one and done at the 2026 ACC Tournament. The 19th-ranked Tar Heels lost 80-79 to Clemson in the quarterfinals at Spectrum Center, bowing out in a rough second-half showing against the Tigers. UNC (24-8) trailed by as many as 18 points after halftime and Clemson (23-10) used a strong performance from its bench to fend off the Heels and earn a spot against Duke (30-2) in the semifinals. The Tigers outscored UNC 29-5 in bench points and iced the game with free throws. UNC was 10 of 17 (59%) from the free-throw line. HIGHLIGHTS: UNC basketball vs Clemson score, live updates: Tar Heels trail at halftime For the fourth time in a row, the Tar Heels lost as a No. 4 seed, dropping its second game in program history against Clemson in the ACC tournament. UNC basketball strong finish not enough vs Clemson The Tar Heels surged with 30 points in the final 7-plus minutes, but it wasn't enough against Clemson. UNC made five shots in a row, including four 3-pointers to make it a one-point game with 3.2 seconds left. But a poor start to the second half cost the Tar Heels the chance for a rematch against Duke. Henri Veesaar scored, but struggled with turnovers for Tar Heels vs Tigers UNC starting center Henri Veesaar was the Tar Heels’ top scorer and rebounder with a career-high 28 points and 17 rebounds against Clemson. But Veesaar had a career-worst six turnovers against Clemson, accounting for more than half of the Tar Heels’ miscues against the Tigers. In a low-possession, one-point game, each turnover proved critical. Jestin Porter, Clemson take command in first half vs UNC Jestin Porter had nine of Clemson’s 15 bench points in the first half and the Tigers used a 19-5 stretch to take a nine-point lead as UNC missed nine of 10 shots late in the first half. The Tar Heels struggled to defend ball screens, miscommunicating on switches and failing to fight over screens. UNC also struggled with its offensive movement, with Veesaar and Stevenson combining for 21 points in the first half. UNC trailed 39-31 at halftime and Clemson used a 22-12 start to the second half to swell its lead to 18. Rodd Baxley covers North Carolina Tar Heels athletics for The Fayetteville Observer as part of the USA TODAY Network. Follow his ACC coverage on X/Twitter or

The First Team All-ACC freshman was having a historic year before surgery ended his season in February, and without him, the Tar Heels enter the tournament with consecutive losses and major questions. Without Wilson in the lineup, this squad drops from 21st overall to 32nd, per Hoop Explorer, with the defense dropping from 20th to 40th. EvanMiya dropped the Tar Heels outside his top 45 without Wilson. Since Feb. 14, UNC ranks 38th on Bart Torvik. Wilson is obviously a big loss. Henri Veesaar is the player who has to fill the void. The junior center put up 28 points and 17 rebounds in the loss to Clemson in the ACC Tournament and is a legitimate NBA-caliber interior presence who creates matchup problems by being able to score in the post, step out on the perimeter, and rebound at an elite rate. He and Seth Trimble are the most reliable contributors left. The rest of the supporting cast is a concern. The bench has combined for single-digit scoring in each of the last two games, and the second unit has not given Hubert Davis anything meaningful to work with when the starters are struggling. The three-point defense is the other genuine problem. UNC ranks outside the top 200 nationally in opponent three-point percentage, and Clemson exploited it by shooting nearly 50% from deep in the ACC quarterfinals. Any opponent that can spread the floor and shoot will find open looks against this scheme. modelTrace.content:
Now I have good information. Let me summarize what I've learned:

**North Carolina (6 seed):**
- Record: 24-8
- KenPom: 29th, Torvik: 27th
- Adjusted offense: 121.5 (33rd), Adjusted defense: 100.6 (around 34th)
- Major injury: Caleb Wilson (star freshman, All-ACC first team, projected top-5 NBA draft pick) is OUT for the season with a broken thumb
- UNC has gone 5-3 without Wilson
- Wilson led the team in all basic stats during the regular season
- Betting line: UNC is only a 2.5-point favorite (-150 moneyline)

**VCU (11 seed):**
- Record: 27-7 (actually 26-7 in the ratings, but 27-7 after winning A10 tournament)
- KenPom: 46th, Torvik: 51st
- Adjusted offense: 120 (45th), Adjusted defense: 103 (82nd)
- Won the A10 tournament championship, beating Dayton 70-62
- Repeated as A10 tournament champions under first-year coach Phil Martelli Jr.
- Freshman guard Nyk Lewis had 17 points and 11 rebounds in the championship game
- VCU is surging - won the A10 tournament

**Key factors:**
1. UNC's star player Caleb Wilson is out for the season - this is a massive blow
2. UNC has struggled without Wilson (5-3 record)
3. VCU is coming in hot after winning the A10 tournament
4. The betting spread is only 2.5 points - very tight for a 6 vs 11 matchup
5. UNC has a slight analytical edge (27-29th vs 46-51st), but the injury significantly closes that gap
6. VCU has momentum and confidence from winning their conference tournament

This is a classic 6/11 upset scenario. The 11-seed VCU has:
- A surging team coming off a conference tournament championship


- An opponent missing their best player
- A very tight betting spread (only 2.5 points)
- A solid analytical profile (46-51st is not bad for an 11-seed)

The injury to Wilson completely changes the calculus. UNC's 5-3 record without him shows they're vulnerable, and VCU's momentum from the A10 tournament gives them confidence. The tight spread (only 2.5 points for a 6 vs 11) suggests the market sees this as very competitive.

I'm leaning toward VCU pulling the upset. The combination of UNC's injury, VCU's momentum, and the narrow spread points to a potential 11-seed victory.

result.title: Breakdown Series | Episode 26: VCU's Havoc Defense - YouTube
result.snippet: In this episode, we're breaking down Shaka Smart's Havoc Defense , the relentless pressure system that carried VCU from mid-major obscurity all the way to the Final Four.

result.title: ESPNInsider: How 'havoc' fuels VCU's success | VCU Ram Nation
result.snippet: 'Havoc' as a disruptive defense Think of Havoc's three pressure defenses as being like three devastating pitches of a baseball pitcher: " Pitch 1 is "Fist," the Rams' fastball: VCU's man-to-man full-court press is designed to put uncomfortable pressure on the opponent's ball handlers with the general idea of creating unforced turnovers .

result.title: Top Sleeper Schools to Watch in 2026 March Madness - Lineups
result.snippet: To compliment their offense, the Rams defense have excelled at smothering their opponents shots at multiple levels of the court, ranking 62nd in Opposing Effective Field Goal Percentage. While their low Rebound Rate is alarming, the Rams Havoc helps them hide it, forcing their opponents into Turnovers before getting up a a shot attempt.

result.title: Havoc Press Defense in Basketball: Information Explained
result.snippet: The havoc press defense is a basketball defensive strategy which utilizes on-ball pressure and defensive traps, particularly in the backcourt, with a main objective of influencing the offensive team to play at a faster pace than usual, which could then lead to turnovers by that same offensive team.

result.title: VCU Rams vs. Fordham Rams, Picks and Prediction for Tuesday, February 3 ...
result.snippet: Forcing turnovers has always been a staple of the VCU defense , though they rank 116th (12.5 per game) in forcing turnovers and they're not using as much pressure ( Havoc ) defense as they used to. According to KenPom, the Rams rank 48th overall, 38th in Adjusted Offensive Efficiency and 95th in Adjusted Defense . Fordham picks up big road win over GW

result.text: UNC vs Clemson in 2026 ACC Tournament: Tar Heels don't complete comeback Skip to main content Home NCAAB NCAAW MLB Women's Sports NFL NBA NCAAF WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Olympics Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers UNC basketball can't complete comeback vs Clemson in ACC tournament Rodd Baxley Fayetteville Observer CHARLOTTE — UNC basketball is one and done at the 2026 ACC Tournament. The 19th-ranked Tar Heels lost 80-79 to Clemson in the quarterfinals at Spectrum Center, bowing out in a rough second-half showing against the Tigers. UNC (24-8) trailed by as many as 18 points after halftime and Clemson (23-10) used a strong performance from its bench to fend off the Heels and earn a spot against Duke (30-2) in the semifinals. The Tigers outscored UNC 29-5 in bench points and iced the game with free throws. UNC was 10 of 17 (59%) from the free-throw line. HIGHLIGHTS: UNC basketball vs Clemson score, live updates: Tar Heels trail at halftime For the fourth time in a row, the Tar Heels lost as a No. 4 seed, dropping its second game in program history against Clemson in the ACC tournament. UNC basketball strong finish not enough vs Clemson The Tar Heels surged with 30 points in the final 7-plus minutes, but it wasn't enough against Clemson. UNC made five shots in a row, including four 3-pointers to make it a one-point game with 3.2 seconds left. But a poor start to the second half cost the Tar Heels the chance for a rematch against Duke. Henri Veesaar scored, but struggled with turnovers for Tar Heels vs Tigers UNC starting center Henri Veesaar was the Tar Heels’ top scorer and rebounder with a career-high 28 points and 17 rebounds against Clemson. But Veesaar had a career-worst six turnovers against Clemson, accounting for more than half of the Tar Heels’ miscues against the Tigers. In a low-possession, one-point game, each turnover proved critical. Jestin Porter, Clemson take command in first half vs UNC Jestin Porter had nine of Clemson’s 15 bench points in the first half and the Tigers used a 19-5 stretch to take a nine-point lead as UNC missed nine of 10 shots late in the first half. The Tar Heels struggled to defend ball screens, miscommunicating on switches and failing to fight over screens. UNC also struggled with its offensive movement, with Veesaar and Stevenson combining for 21 points in the first half. UNC trailed 39-31 at halftime and Clemson used a 22-12 start to the second half to swell its lead to 18. Rodd Baxley covers North Carolina Tar Heels athletics for The Fayetteville Observer as part of the USA TODAY Network. Follow his ACC coverage on X/Twitter or
